channel.title: +Marriage on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +Marriage on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Bacon on marriage
Rilke
item.description: From Rilke’s ‘Letters to a Young Poet’: The point of marriage is not to create a quick commonality by tearing down all boundaries; on the contrary, a good marriage is one in which each partner appoints the other to be the guardian of his solitude, and thus they show each other the greatest possible trust.+++(5)+++ A merging of two people is an impossibility, and where it seems to exist, it is a hemming-in, a mutual consent that robs one party or both parties of their fullest freedom and development.
